# Award

Portfolio Data

Back to Award Search

## EPIC (Electromagnetic Personnel Interdiction Control)

### Awardee

#### INVOCON, INC. 19221 INTERSTATE 45 S STE 530  
CONROE, TX, 77385-8746  
USA

**Award Year:** 2005

**UEI:** LZQCQX2HPGN5
**HUBZone Owned:** No

**Woman Owned:** No

**Socially and Economically Disadvantaged:** No

**Congressional District:** 8

Tagged as:

SBIR

Phase II

### Awarding Agency

DOD

Branch: NAVY

Total Award Amount: $749,843

**Contract Number:** M67854-05-C-6509

**Agency Tracking Number:** N032-0874

**Solicitation Topic Code:** N03-163

**Solicitation Number:** 2003\.2

## Abstract

The Marine Corps has indicated a need for a new technology that could form the basis of non-lethal and non-destructive weapons. The purpose of these weapons would be to render an adversary either incapable or unwilling to resist installation of captive restraints. This incapacitation must last for a period of time needed to subdue the individual and by means of restraint installation preclude any further hostile action. This requirement dictates that when the weapon is used to subdue an enemy, no permanent damage of any kind to the target will be tolerated.
In addition, the requirement states that the weapon must be able to attack targets through walls without damage to the walls or to the target. Invocon, Inc. has completed a Phase 1 and a Phase 1 Option. The EPIC concept was refined and documented as were the anticipated effects on human targets. Research was completed that defined the human physiology that was to be the specific target of the EPIC stimulus as well as estimations of the radiated energies needed to create the desired non-destructive effects. The Phase 2 proposal seeks to verify the initial concept definition and provide unassailable evidence of "cause and effect" of the EPIC stimulus on living animal tissue that sufficiently replicates human anatomy. Further, it seeks to prove that the effects of the EPIC stimulus can be endured by the target with complete assurance that no permanent damage will result.

## Award Schedule

1. **2003**  
   Solicitation Year
2. **2005**  
   Award Year
3. **June 9, 2005**

results.title: Contract Data | SAM.gov
excerpts:
# Contract Award Data

**To manage and run reports on contract award data, access the** [**DataBank**](https://sam.gov/reports/awards/standard) **. To search for specific contract award data, use the "Contracting" domain and select "Contract Awards."**

Contract data provides detailed information on all contract actions that are required to be reported by federal agencies. These are actions with an estimated value of $10,000 or more.

## SAM Search

SAM Search

Dropdown 1

Select Domain... Contract Opportunities Contract Awards

Text Field

## How do Contract Awards search filters function?

If you have a specific searchable phrase (e.g.: small business contracts, information technology, SHIP AND MARINE EQUIPMENT, termination for cause), you can use the options mentioned below:

Keyword Search > Simple Search > Any Words

Keyword Search > Simple Search > All Words

Keyword Search > Simple Search > Exact Phrase
If you intend to search using a SQL query-like conditions, then use Keyword Search > Search Editor

If you intend to search using the pre-designed search parameters/filters, then select enter/select one or many values from the following:

**Contracting Federal Organizations:** Offers Department and Sub-tier selections

**Entity:** Offers Entity Name (Legal Business Name), Unique Entity Identifier and CAGE/NCAGE selections

**Socio-Economic Status:** Offers Entity socio-economic credits as selections

**Product or Service Information:** Offers NAICS and PSC selections

**Place of Performance:** Offers Country, State, City, Zip Code and Congressional District selections

**Contract Identification:** Offers PIID, Referenced IDV PIID, Solicitation ID and Document Type selections. PIID, Referenced IDV PIID and Solicitation ID allow partial and wildcard searches. E.g.: W58, GS\*89.

**Dates:** Offers Date Signed, IDV Last Date to Order and Contract Fiscal Year selections
**Competition:** Offers Extent Competed, Type of Set Aside and Contract Opportunities Notice selections

**Status:** Active (not Closed Out CARs) and Inactive (Closed Out CARs) selections

**NOTE:** The Keyword Search has the ability to find a match by searching across several data elements.

## What data is in Contract Awards?

**Non-fed users,** will have access to all the Award (Delivery/Task Order, Purchase Order, Definitive Contract, BPA Call), IDV (FSS, GWAC, BOA, BPA, IDC) and Other Transaction (Order, Agreement, IDV) data, except for the DoD Unrevealed (within the 90 days from the signed date) data.

**Federal users** , will have access to all the Award (Delivery/Task Order, Purchase Order, Definitive Contract, BPA Call), IDV (FSS, GWAC, BOA, BPA, IDC) and Other Transaction (Order, Agreement, IDV) data, except for the DoD Unrevealed (within the 90 days from the signed date) data.

## EPIC (Electromagnetic Personnel Interdiction Control)

### Awardee

#### INVOCON, INC. 19221 INTERSTATE 45 S STE 530  
CONROE, TX, 77385-8746  
USA

**Award Year:** 2003

**UEI:** LZQCQX2HPGN5
**HUBZone Owned:** No

**Woman Owned:** No

**Socially and Economically Disadvantaged:** No

**Congressional District:** 8

Tagged as:

SBIR

Phase I

### Awarding Agency

DOD

Branch: NAVY

Total Award Amount: $69,977

**Contract Number:** M67854-04-C-1013

**Agency Tracking Number:** N032-0874

**Solicitation Topic Code:** N/A

**Solicitation Number:** N/A

## Abstract

The Marine Corps has a requirement for a non-destructive stun weapon that would render a hostile war fighter ineffective for a period of time. There is significant political and military interest in such a capability. In addition, the ability to remotelyincapacitate a human being without permanent damage would be a landmark event in the field of civil law enforcement. The desirability of waging bloodless war to counter threats to national security is virtually limitless since both military and civilauthority could determine a priori when and if loss of human life is necessary.
IVC proposes to investigate the use of beamed RF energy to excite and interrupt the normal process of human hearing and equilibrium. The focus will be in two areas. (1)Interruption of the mechanical transduction process by which sound and position (relative to gravity) are converted to messages that are processed by the brain. (2) Interruption of the chemical engine which sustains the proper operation of the nerve cellsthat respond to the mechanical transduction mechanisms referenced in item (1). Interruption of either or both of these processes has been clinically shown to produce complete disorientation and confusion. Second order effects would be extreme motionsickness.
